title: 신용장(L/C)
queries: letter of credit document fields applicant beneficiary issuing bank expiry date latest date of shipment UCP 600
International Chamber of Commerce UCP 600 documentary credits letter of credit
letter of credit applicant beneficiary issuing bank nominated bank documents required charges

sources.summary: Letter of credit is a bank-issued payment guarantee for trade; LCs are regulated by UCP 600 and transmitted through SWIFT MT700 messages; key features include irrevocability, documentary nature, compliance, payment mechanism, and autonomy.
Defines roles such as applicant, beneficiary, issuing bank, nominated/advising bank and explains document presentation, expiry/last shipment timing, required documents, and charges concepts.
UCP is the ICC-standardized rule set for documentary credits; current UCP 600 took effect in 2007 and is used internationally for letter-of-credit practice.

field_evidence.rationale: LCs are formal bank-issued documentary credits commonly transmitted as structured SWIFT MT700-style messages.
Source describes applicant, beneficiary, issuing bank and nominated/advising bank roles.
UCP/MT700-style documentary credit practice supports mapping visible numbered clauses to expiry, amount, shipment, goods, documents and conditions.

limitations: 웹 검색은 문서 의미와 faker 근사에만 사용했고, 템플릿에 보이지 않는 필드는 추가하지 않았다.
SWIFT field-code 세부 공식 사양은 공개 검색 결과 접근성이 제한되어 전체 이미지의 visible field code와 일반 LC/UCP 근거를 함께 사용했다.
